description: §49.9 — a bare `continue` in a position not enclosed by any `while`, `do...while`, or `for...of` loop fires E-LOOP-002. The SPEC's own worked example (§49.9 E-LOOP-002 block); the `continue` twin of loop-001-break-outside. W-PROGRAM-001 co-fires incidentally (bare markup, no `<program>` shell).
rationale: §49.9 E-LOOP-002: the compiler SHALL reject `continue` that appears outside any enclosing loop. `continue` may only appear inside a `while`, `do...while`, or `for...of` loop body.
